text.title: Accessibility Testing Process Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Accessibility Testing Process methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Audit report MUST include findings from all four layers: automated, manual keyboard, AT testing (≥2 screen readers), and disabled-user feedback (or explicit waiver with reason).

rationale.source: WAI evaluation methodology (WAI-EM) + Deque coverage research

rule.rationale: Each layer catches different defects; skipping any layer hides issues.

rule.statement: AT testing covers at least two screen readers — minimum: one desktop (NVDA or JAWS) + one mobile (VoiceOver iOS or TalkBack).
rule.rationale: Different SRs surface different bugs; single-SR audits miss platform-specific failures.

rule.statement: Severity per finding is one of: blocker / major / minor / informational. Free-form severity strings are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Closed enum enables triage and dashboarding.

rule.statement: Findings sourced from disabled-user feedback are tagged `user_panel=true` and weighted at ≥1.5× synthetic findings during prioritisation.
rule.rationale: User-panel findings have higher real-world impact than synthetic ones.

rule.statement: Every finding attaches evidence: scanner output URL, video clip, or session note. Findings with no evidence are forbidden.
If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-a11y-testing.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
